Issue: PyTorch 2.9 compatibility issue with torch.Tensor input in compute_shape_offset function
Decision: Wrap spatial_shape in tuple() before passing to np.array() to ensure compatibility with PyTorch tensors
Rationale: The direct caller in monai/transforms/spatial/functional.py constructs an in_spatial_size as a torch.Tensor and passes it to compute_shape_offset, which relies on non-tuple sequence indexing protocol removed in PyTorch 2.9. Wrapping spatial_shape in tuple() routes through __iter__, which is stable across PyTorch versions, and produces 0-d scalar tensors that NumPy consumes correctly.
Author: williams145
Description: compute_shape_offset function in monai/data/utils.py and its caller in monai/transforms/spatial/functional.py
Time stamp: 2026-04-09T11:17:51Z (created), 2026-04-09T16:13:01Z (comment by williams145), 2026-04-09T19:59:10Z (comment by ericspod), 2026-04-09T23:59:10Z (comment by williams145)
Cost: Minimal code change (one-character modification) and addition of new unit tests
Risk: Potential breakage of existing code relying on torch.Tensor input in compute_shape_offset function

Argument.author: williams145
Argument.argument: The fix is to wrap spatial_shape in tuple() before passing it to np.array(). This routes through __iter__, which is stable across all PyTorch versions, and produces 0-d scalar tensors that NumPy consumes correctly.

Argument.author: ericspod
Argument.argument: PR #8814 has been merged. The pybind11 blocker should now be resolved, this should mean that CI can be re-triggered when convenient.

Justification: The issue involves a compatibility fix for PyTorch 2.9 and NumPy tensor handling, which is a general software bug unrelated to core ML lifecycle stages like data preparation, modeling, or deployment.
